name: Sulayman ibn Surad
title: Leader of Tawwabin
shortDescription: A Shia leader in Kufa who missed helping the Imam on Ashura and, to compensate, led the great Tawwabin uprising and was martyred.
fullStory:
Sulayman ibn Surad al-Khuza'i was a prominent figure, a companion of the Prophet (PBUH), and a well-known Shia in Kufa. He was the first to gather the Shias in his house after Mu'awiya's death to write letters inviting Imam Hussain (AS) to Kufa.

However, when Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad conquered Kufa through oppression and intimidation, Sulayman failed to reach Karbala and missed the opportunity to help the Imam. After the event of Ashura, he and many other Kufan Shias felt intense regret.

To compensate for this great sin, Sulayman took leadership of a group called 'Tawwabin' (The Penitents). They swore to either avenge Imam Hussain's (AS) blood or be killed.

Four years after Ashura, the Tawwabin army led by Sulayman marched towards Sham and engaged in a fierce battle with the massive Umayyad army in an area called 'Ayn al-Warda'. Sulayman fought bravely and was finally martyred by an arrow strike.
quoteArabic: اللَّهُمَّ ارْحَمْ حُسَيْناً الشَّهِيدَ ابْنَ الشَّهِيدِ... اللَّهُمَّ إِنّا نُشْهِدُكَ أَنّا عَلى دِينِهِمْ...
quote: (His prayer at the Imam's grave:) O God! Forgive us for not helping him... We are upon the same religion that Hussain was killed for.
